diffs.after:
# .github/workflows/smoke-test.yml
name: Smoke Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [ "master" ]
  pull_request:

jobs:

  dry-run:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: dry-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-basic --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "=== Dry Run ===" || { echo "FAIL: '=== Dry Run ===' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-tools
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-tools --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Tools ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Tools ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "read_file" || { echo "FAIL: 'read_file' not found in tools section"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-skill
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Skill ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Skill ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -A1 "--- Skill ---" | grep -q "(none)" && { echo "FAIL: skill section is '(none)'"; exit 1; } || true

    - name: dry-memory
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-memory --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Memory ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Memory ---' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-files
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-files --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Files ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Files ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context.txt" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context.txt' not found in files section"; exit 1; }

  providers:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: provider-anthropic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-anthropic -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: provider-openai
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-openai -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: provider-opencode
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-opencode -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  tools:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
      TAVILY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TAVILY_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: tool-list-dir
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-list-dir -p "List the files in the current directory and tell me what you see.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-read-file
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-read-file -p "Read the file .github/smoke-agents/smoke-context.txt and tell me its contents.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-write-file
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-write-file -p "Write the text 'smoke-write-ok' to the file /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        grep -q "smoke-write-ok" /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt || { echo "FAIL: write file content not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-edit-file
      run: |
        echo "original-content" > /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-edit-file -p "Edit the file /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt: replace 'original-content' with 'edited-content'")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        grep -q "edited-content" /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt || { echo "FAIL: edited content not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-run-cmd
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-run-cmd -p "Run the shell command: echo hello-smoke")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "hello-smoke" || { echo "FAIL: 'hello-smoke' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-url-fetch
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-url-fetch -p "Fetch the URL https://example.com and tell me the page title.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Example Domain" || { echo "FAIL: 'Example Domain' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-web-search
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-web-search -p "Search the web for 'axe cli golang' and summarize what you find.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  config-sections:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: cfg-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-basic -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-skill
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" -p "Follow your instructions.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-skill-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-skill-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-memory
      run: |
        ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory -p "Remember this: memory-smoke-test"
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Memory ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Memory ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "memory-smoke-test" || { echo "FAIL: memory entry not found in dry-run output"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-files
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-files -p "What does the context file say?")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-subagents
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-subagents -p "Delegate to your sub-agent and report what it says.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "sub-agent-ok" || { echo "FAIL: 'sub-agent-ok' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-params
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-params -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  piped-input:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: pipe-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(echo "pipe-smoke-input" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic)
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: pipe-with-prompt
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(echo "context: pipe-smoke-context" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic -p "Repeat back the context you were given.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

parts.text:
Create the file `.github/workflows/smoke-test.yml` in the repo at `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe`.

This is a GitHub Actions workflow for smoke-testing the `axe` binary using real LLM provider calls. Here is the complete specification:

---

## Triggers
- `push` to `master`
- `pull_request`

Same triggers as `.github/workflows/go.yml`.

---

## Jobs Overview

5 jobs total:
1. `dry-run` — no API keys needed, free
2. `providers` — tests Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenCode connectivity
3. `tools` — exercises all 7 built-in tools via OpenCode
4. `config-sections` — verifies TOML config parsing
5. `piped-input` — verifies stdin piping

All jobs:
- Run on `ubuntu-latest`
- Build the binary first: `go build -o axe .`
- Use Go 1.24 (`actions/setup-go@v5`)
- Run from the repo root
- Use `--agents-dir .github/smoke-agents` for all `axe run` invocations

---

## Job 1: `dry-run`

**No secrets needed.**

Steps (after checkout + go setup + build):

1. **dry-basic** — baseline dry-run:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-basic --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `=== Dry Run ===`

2. **dry-tools** — tools section populated:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-tools --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `--- Tools ---`, stdout does NOT contain `(none)` in the tools section (check that `read_file` appears in stdout)

3. **dry-skill** — skill section not `(none)`:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `--- Skill ---`, stdout does NOT contain `(none)` after `--- Skill ---`

4. **dry-memory** — memory section present:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-memory --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `--- Memory ---`

5. **dry-files** — files section not `(none)`:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-files --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `--- Files ---`, stdout contains `smoke-context.txt`

For assertions, use shell `grep` with `-q` flag and fail with a descriptive message if the assertion fails. Pattern:
```bash
OUTPUT=$(./axe run ...)
echo "$OUTPUT"
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "expected string" || { echo "FAIL: expected string not found"; exit 1; }
```

---

## Job 2: `providers`

**Secrets needed:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENCODE_API_KEY`

Inject secrets as env vars on the job level:
```yaml
env:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
  OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
  OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
```

Steps (after checkout + go setup + build):

1. **provider-anthropic**:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-anthropic -p "Reply with only the word: pong"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

2. **provider-openai**:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-openai -p "Reply with only the word: pong"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

3. **provider-opencode**:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-opencode -p "Reply with only the word: pong"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

For non-empty assertion:
```bash
OUTPUT=$(./axe run ...)
echo "$OUTPUT"
[ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }
```

---

## Job 3: `tools`

**Secrets needed:** `OPENCODE_API_KEY`, `TAVILY_API_KEY`

Inject on job level:
```yaml
env:
  OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
  TAVILY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TAVILY_API_KEY }}
```

Steps (after checkout + go setup + build):

1. **tool-list-dir** — list_directory tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-list-dir -p "List the files in the current directory and tell me what you see."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

2. **tool-read-file** — read_file tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-read-file -p "Read the file .github/smoke-agents/smoke-context.txt and tell me its contents."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `smoke-context-loaded`

3. **tool-write-file** — write_file tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-write-file -p "Write the text 'smoke-write-ok' to the file /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, file `/tmp/smoke-write-test.txt` exists and contains `smoke-write-ok`
   ```bash
   grep -q "smoke-write-ok" /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt || { echo "FAIL: write file content not found"; exit 1; }
   ```

4. **tool-edit-file** — edit_file tool:
   First create a file to edit:
   ```bash
   echo "original-content" > /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt
   ```
   Then run:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-edit-file -p "Edit the file /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt: replace 'original-content' with 'edited-content'"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, file `/tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt` contains `edited-content`

5. **tool-run-cmd** — run_command tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-run-cmd -p "Run the shell command: echo hello-smoke"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `hello-smoke`

6. **tool-url-fetch** — url_fetch tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-url-fetch -p "Fetch the URL https://example.com and tell me the page title."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `Example Domain`

7. **tool-web-search** — web_search tool:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-web-search -p "Search the web for 'axe cli golang' and summarize what you find."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

---

## Job 4: `config-sections`

**Secrets needed:** `OPENCODE_API_KEY`

Inject on job level:
```yaml
env:
  OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
```

Steps (after checkout + go setup + build):

1. **cfg-basic** — minimal config:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-basic -p "Reply with only the word: pong"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

2. **cfg-skill** — skill config section:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" -p "Follow your instructions."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `smoke-skill-loaded`

3. **cfg-memory** — memory config section (two sequential runs):
   Run 1 (seed memory):
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory -p "Remember this: memory-smoke-test"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0
   
   Run 2 (verify memory persisted via dry-run):
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory --dry-run -p "ping"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `--- Memory ---`, stdout does NOT contain `(none)` immediately after `--- Memory ---` (i.e., memory has content)
   
   For the memory assertion, check that the memory section is non-empty:
   ```bash
   OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory --dry-run -p "ping")
   echo "$OUTPUT"
   echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "memory-smoke-test" || { echo "FAIL: memory entry not found in dry-run output"; exit 1; }
   ```

4. **cfg-files** — files config section:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-files -p "What does the context file say?"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `smoke-context-loaded`

5. **cfg-subagents** — sub_agents config section:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-subagents -p "Delegate to your sub-agent and report what it says."
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout contains `sub-agent-ok`

6. **cfg-params** — params config section:
   ```
   ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-params -p "Reply with only the word: pong"
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

---

## Job 5: `piped-input`

**Secrets needed:** `OPENCODE_API_KEY`

Inject on job level:
```yaml
env:
  OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
```

Steps (after checkout + go setup + build):

1. **pipe-basic** — basic stdin piping:
   ```bash
   OUTPUT=$(echo "pipe-smoke-input" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic)
   echo "$OUTPUT"
   [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

2. **pipe-with-prompt** — stdin + explicit prompt:
   ```bash
   OUTPUT=$(echo "context: pipe-smoke-context" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic -p "Repeat back the context you were given.")
   echo "$OUTPUT"
   [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }
   ```
   Assert: exit 0, stdout is non-empty

---

## Important Implementation Notes

1. **Each step should capture output and echo it** before asserting, so failures are debuggable in CI logs.

2. **Use `set -e` is NOT needed** — each step is its own shell invocation in GitHub Actions and already fails on non-zero exit. But explicit `|| { echo "FAIL: ..."; exit 1; }` patterns are preferred for clarity.

3. **The `go build` step** in each job:
   ```yaml
   - name: Build axe
     run: go build -o axe .
   ```

4. **Checkout and Go setup** are standard:
   ```yaml
   - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   - name: Set up Go
     uses: actions/setup-go@v5
     with:
       go-version: '1.24'
   ```

5. **Each assertion step should have a descriptive `name:`** in the YAML so failures are easy to identify in the Actions UI.

6. **The workflow file header:**
   ```yaml
   # .github/workflows/smoke-test.yml
   name: Smoke Tests
   ```

7. **No `needs:` dependencies between jobs** — all 5 jobs run in parallel (they're independent).

8. **`continue-on-error: false`** (default) — a failing step fails the job.

Write the complete `.github/workflows/smoke-test.yml` file. Do not create any other files.

input.content:
# .github/workflows/smoke-test.yml
name: Smoke Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [ "master" ]
  pull_request:

jobs:

  dry-run:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: dry-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-basic --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "=== Dry Run ===" || { echo "FAIL: '=== Dry Run ===' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-tools
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-tools --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Tools ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Tools ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "read_file" || { echo "FAIL: 'read_file' not found in tools section"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-skill
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Skill ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Skill ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -A1 "--- Skill ---" | grep -q "(none)" && { echo "FAIL: skill section is '(none)'"; exit 1; } || true

    - name: dry-memory
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-memory --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Memory ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Memory ---' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: dry-files
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents dry-files --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Files ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Files ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context.txt" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context.txt' not found in files section"; exit 1; }

  providers:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: provider-anthropic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-anthropic -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: provider-openai
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-openai -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: provider-opencode
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents provider-opencode -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  tools:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
      TAVILY_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TAVILY_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: tool-list-dir
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-list-dir -p "List the files in the current directory and tell me what you see.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-read-file
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-read-file -p "Read the file .github/smoke-agents/smoke-context.txt and tell me its contents.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-write-file
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-write-file -p "Write the text 'smoke-write-ok' to the file /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        grep -q "smoke-write-ok" /tmp/smoke-write-test.txt || { echo "FAIL: write file content not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-edit-file
      run: |
        echo "original-content" > /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-edit-file -p "Edit the file /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt: replace 'original-content' with 'edited-content'")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        grep -q "edited-content" /tmp/smoke-edit-test.txt || { echo "FAIL: edited content not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-run-cmd
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-run-cmd -p "Run the shell command: echo hello-smoke")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "hello-smoke" || { echo "FAIL: 'hello-smoke' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-url-fetch
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-url-fetch -p "Fetch the URL https://example.com and tell me the page title.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Example Domain" || { echo "FAIL: 'Example Domain' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: tool-web-search
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents tool-web-search -p "Search the web for 'axe cli golang' and summarize what you find.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  config-sections:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: cfg-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-basic -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-skill
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-skill --skill "$(pwd)/.github/smoke-agents/skills/smoke-skill/SKILL.md" -p "Follow your instructions.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-skill-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-skill-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-memory
      run: |
        ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory -p "Remember this: memory-smoke-test"
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-memory --dry-run -p "ping")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "--- Memory ---" || { echo "FAIL: '--- Memory ---' not found"; exit 1; }
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "memory-smoke-test" || { echo "FAIL: memory entry not found in dry-run output"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-files
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-files -p "What does the context file say?")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "smoke-context-loaded" || { echo "FAIL: 'smoke-context-loaded' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-subagents
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-subagents -p "Delegate to your sub-agent and report what it says.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "sub-agent-ok" || { echo "FAIL: 'sub-agent-ok' not found"; exit 1; }

    - name: cfg-params
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents cfg-params -p "Reply with only the word: pong")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

  piped-input:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      OPENCODE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENCODE_API_KEY }}
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version: '1.24'

    - name: Build axe
      run: go build -o axe .

    - name: pipe-basic
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(echo "pipe-smoke-input" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic)
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

    - name: pipe-with-prompt
      run: |
        OUTPUT=$(echo "context: pipe-smoke-context" | ./axe run --agents-dir .github/smoke-agents pipe-basic -p "Repeat back the context you were given.")
        echo "$OUTPUT"
        [ -n "$OUTPUT" ] || { echo "FAIL: empty output"; exit 1; }

parts.text:
Created `.github/workflows/smoke-test.yml` with all 5 jobs:

- **`dry-run`** — 5 steps testing `--dry-run` output assertions (no secrets required)
- **`providers`** — 3 steps verifying Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenCode connectivity
- **`tools`** — 7 steps exercising all built-in tools via OpenCode
- **`config-sections`** — 6 steps verifying TOML config parsing (basic, skill, memory, files, sub-agents, params)
- **`piped-input`** — 2 steps verifying stdin piping (basic and with explicit prompt)

All jobs run in parallel on `ubuntu-latest`, build the binary first, use `--agents-dir .github/smoke-agents`, and follow the capture-then-assert pattern for debuggable CI logs.
